summary: End-to-end testing of Global Intel Desk SaaS app. All 18 backend pytest tests pass (health, auth register/login/me/guards, contact form mocked email, itinerary CRUD, AI risk assessment via Claude Sonnet 4.5, refresh-alerts, debrief, dashboard stats, delete). All 12 frontend Playwright flows pass (home/services/about render, protected route redirect, login, dashboard, new itinerary creation with AI assessment, debrief save, delete, logout, contact form submit, register flow).
critical_code_review_comments: Frontend NewItinerary.jsx purpose select sends 'Business', 'Diplomatic', etc. (capitalised + spaces like 'Humanitarian / NGO') while backend ItineraryCreate.purpose is a free-form str — works today but if backend ever validates enum values like 'business|diplomatic|tourism|humanitarian' it will break. Consider aligning option values to the documented set.
server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown'); switch to lifespan context manager for FastAPI >=0.110.
JWT_SECRET in /app/backend/.env is a placeholder ('replace_in_prod'); ensure rotated for production.
RESEND_API_KEY empty is intentional per request (mocked). No issue.

AI assessment is synchronous and can take 30-60s during itinerary submit — UI shows 'Provisioning mission...' but consider adding a progress indicator or optimistic redirect with background polling for better UX on slow LLM responses.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed users/itineraries/contacts; itinerary delete fixture cleans up created records.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all endpoints incl. AI assess (Claude 4.5, ~30s). Frontend tested via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL public URL. AI assessment is invoked automatically on itinerary creation in UI; allow 60s timeout for navigation to detail page. RESEND_API_KEY intentionally empty -> /api/contact returns email_dispatched=false — do not flag.
rca of the issue: No bugs identified. One self-fixed test bug: register test asserted email case preserved, but server lowercases emails on register/login (correct behavior) — assertion adjusted to email.lower().
